_doc: Pre-scored evergreen topic inventory. Built by build_topic_bank.py — edit TOPICS there and re-run, don't hand-edit this file. Weighted total = 3*hook+2*mystery+2*share+2*visual+2*cred+search+series (max 130). Greenlight >=85; cred<6 -> graveyard; visual<7 -> long-form only. Grow this toward 300-500 via the CONTENT_ENGINE sourcing funnel; every entry must be a REAL event (no padding).

topics.title: A 2,000-Year-Old Computer Found in a Shipwreck
topics.hook: Ancient Greeks built a gear computer we couldn't match for 1,000 years.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Antikythera mechanism, ~2nd c. BCE analog computer; function reconstructed.

topics.title: A Book No One Has Ever Been Able to Read
topics.hook: 600 years old, fully illustrated, in a language that doesn't exist.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Voynich manuscript undeciphered; carbon-dated ~15th c.

topics.title: An Entire Colony Vanished, Leaving One Word
topics.hook: 120 settlers gone — only 'CROATOAN' carved in wood.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Roanoke colonists missing by 1590; 'CROATOAN' carving documented; fate is THEORY.

topics.title: A Ghost Ship Found With Everyone Gone
topics.hook: Hot food, full cargo, not a soul aboard.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Mary Celeste found adrift 1872, crew never found; cause THEORY.

topics.title: Three Lighthouse Keepers Vanished Without a Trace
topics.hook: The lamp was out, the door locked, the men gone.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1900 Flannan Isles keepers vanished; cause is THEORY (rogue wave leading).

topics.title: 129 Men Sailed Into the Arctic and Were Never Seen Alive Again
topics.hook: Two ships, frozen in, slowly vanished.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Franklin's 1845 expedition lost; ships found 2014/2016; details still THEORY.

topics.title: Something Flattened 800 Square Miles of Forest
topics.hook: An explosion 1,000x Hiroshima — with no crater.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1908 Tunguska event; airburst THEORY (most accepted).

topics.title: Nine Hikers Fled Their Tent Into the Snow and Died
topics.hook: They cut their way out half-dressed in -25C — no one knows why.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1959 Dyatlov Pass deaths real; cause THEORY (avalanche model recent, still debated).

topics.title: Australia Lost a War to Birds in 1932
topics.hook: Soldiers vs. 20,000 emus with machine guns — the birds won.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: real 1932 military operation in WA; emus 'won'. Casualty/round figures well documented.

topics.title: Romans Made These and We Still Don't Know Why
topics.hook: Hundreds of bronze objects, zero written explanation.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Roman dodecahedra exist; purpose unknown (THEORY).

topics.title: A Weapon That Burned on Water and We Lost the Recipe
topics.hook: The formula was so secret it died with the empire.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Greek fire real Byzantine weapon; exact recipe lost (THEORY on composition).

topics.title: A Bear Was an Official Soldier in WWII
topics.hook: He carried artillery shells and had a rank.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Wojtek enlisted in the Polish II Corps; carried supplies.

topics.title: In 1518, a Town Danced Itself to Death
topics.hook: Hundreds danced for days — some until they died.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Dancing Plague of 1518 in city records; cause THEORY (ergot vs mass hysteria).

topics.title: A Man Survived an Iron Rod Through His Brain
topics.hook: A 3-foot spike shot through his skull — and he walked away.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Phineas Gage, 1848; personality-change claims partly THEORY.

topics.title: A Lake Exploded and Suffocated a Whole Valley
topics.hook: 1,700 people died in their sleep from an invisible cloud.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1986 Lake Nyos limnic eruption; CO2 release.

topics.title: Factory Workers Painted With Glowing Poison
topics.hook: They licked radioactive brushes — and their bones rotted.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Radium Girls, 1910s-20s; landmark labor/health case.

topics.title: A Wave of Molasses Killed 21 People
topics.hook: A 25-foot wall of syrup tore through Boston at 35 mph.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1919 Boston Molasses Flood; 21 dead, tank failure.

topics.title: Did Two Climbers Reach Everest 29 Years Before Hillary?
topics.hook: One body was found in 1999 — the other, and the camera, are still missing.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Mallory & Irvine vanished 1924; summit question unresolved (THEORY).

topics.title: A Ship Was Crushed by Ice and Everyone Still Survived
topics.hook: Shackleton's open-boat journey is almost unbelievable.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Endurance 1914-17; all survived; wreck found 2022.

topics.title: A City for 20,000 People Hidden Underground
topics.hook: An entire town carved beneath the earth.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Derinkuyu underground city, Cappadocia; capacity estimates vary.

topics.title: A Roman Cup That Changes Color Like Nanotech
topics.hook: 1,600 years ago, Romans used nanoparticles in glass.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Lycurgus Cup uses colloidal gold/silver nanoparticles; dichroic effect real.

topics.title: Roman Concrete Heals Itself — Ours Crumbles
topics.hook: 2,000-year-old harbors are still standing.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Roman concrete's longevity and self-healing (lime clasts) studied/published.

topics.title: The Man Who Was Poisoned, Shot, and Still Wouldn't Die
topics.hook: His murder reads like a horror movie.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Rasputin killed 1916; the 'unkillable' details are partly legend.

topics.title: Three Men Tried to Reach the North Pole by Balloon
topics.hook: Their fate was a mystery for 33 years — until their camera was found.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: S.A. Andree's 1897 balloon expedition; remains/film found 1930.

topics.title: Five Bombers Vanished, Then So Did the Rescue Plane
topics.hook: A whole squadron disappeared off Florida in 1945.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Flight 19 lost 1945; 'Bermuda Triangle' framing is THEORY — likely navigation error.

topics.title: She Was Miles From Land When She Vanished
topics.hook: The most famous pilot on Earth simply disappeared.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Earhart/Noonan lost 1937; resting place THEORY (Nikumaroro leading).

topics.title: A Hijacker Parachuted Into the Night and Vanished
topics.hook: He took the cash, jumped, and was never seen again.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1971 D.B. Cooper hijacking; identity unsolved.

topics.title: A Stone City Built on a Coral Reef in the Pacific
topics.hook: Massive basalt 'logs' stacked in the ocean.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Nan Madol real; how stones were moved is THEORY.

topics.title: A Volcano Caused a Year Without Summer
topics.hook: 1816 had snow in June and crops failed worldwide.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1815 Mt Tambora eruption; 1816 'Year Without a Summer'.

topics.title: A Man Drank Radioactive 'Health Water' Until His Jaw Fell Off
topics.hook: The miracle tonic was slowly killing him.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Eben Byers, Radithor, died 1932.

topics.title: A Man Who Could Eat Almost Anything and Never Get Full
topics.hook: He ate live animals, corks, and a whole meal for 15.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Tarrare documented by military surgeons; some accounts likely exaggerated.

topics.title: He Fought WWII With a Longbow and a Sword
topics.hook: 'Mad Jack' brought a claymore to a world war.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Jack Churchill carried bow and sword; one longbow kill attested.

topics.title: An Explorer Vanished Hunting a Lost City
topics.hook: He walked into the Amazon for 'Z' and never returned.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Fawcett vanished 1925; fate THEORY.

topics.title: A Tidal Wave of Beer Drowned People in London
topics.hook: A burst vat sent 1 million litres into the streets.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1814 London Beer Flood; 8 dead.

topics.title: The Biggest Man-Made Blast Before the Atom Bomb
topics.hook: A ship collision leveled a city in 1917.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1917 Halifax Explosion; ~2,000 dead.

topics.title: A Doctor Removed His Own Appendix in Antarctica
topics.hook: Stranded and dying, he operated on himself.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Rogozov self-appendectomy, 1961.

topics.title: The Father of Film Vanished Off a Moving Train
topics.hook: He made the first movie, then disappeared days before fame.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Le Prince vanished 1890; cause unsolved.

topics.title: A Schooner Ran Aground With No Crew and a Pot of Food
topics.hook: The ghost ship's entire crew was simply gone.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Carroll A. Deering found 1921; crew never found; cause THEORY.

topics.title: A 2,000-Year-Old Jar That Looks Like a Battery
topics.hook: Did ancient people have electricity?
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: the artifacts exist; the 'battery' function is THEORY and widely doubted.

topics.title: Viking Swords Made of Steel Too Advanced for Their Time
topics.hook: Crucible steel that shouldn't have existed in Europe yet.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: +VLFBERH+T swords used high-purity crucible steel; sourcing debated (THEORY).

topics.title: A Broke Man Declared Himself Emperor — and a City Played Along
topics.hook: San Francisco printed his money and obeyed his decrees.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Joshua Norton, 'Emperor of the United States', 1859-1880.

topics.title: People Drilled Holes in Skulls 7,000 Years Ago — and Survived
topics.hook: Healed bone shows many patients lived.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: trepanation is one of the oldest surgeries; survival shown by bone healing.

topics.title: The War That Lasted 38 Minutes
topics.hook: The shortest war in history was over before lunch.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 27 Aug 1896, ~38 min, Britain vs Zanzibar.

topics.title: A Pirate Queen Who Commanded 80,000 Men
topics.hook: She ran the largest pirate fleet in history — and retired rich.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Zheng Yi Sao / Ching Shih led a vast Chinese pirate confederation.

topics.title: The Deadliest Tornado Stayed on the Ground for 3 Hours
topics.hook: One twister crossed three states and killed 695.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1925 Tri-State Tornado; deadliest in US history.

topics.title: The Doctor Mocked for Saying 'Wash Your Hands'
topics.hook: He cut deaths by 90% — and was driven to an asylum.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Ignaz Semmelweis, handwashing, 1840s; rejected in his time.

topics.title: A Wagon Train Trapped in the Snow Faced the Unthinkable
topics.hook: Stranded for months, survivors did what they had to.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Donner Party, 1846-47; cannibalism attested by survivors.

topics.title: Two Cities Went to War Over a Stolen Bucket
topics.hook: Thousands died over a wooden pail in 1325.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1325 Modena vs Bologna; the bucket is still displayed. Death toll figures vary.

topics.title: Mauled by a Bear and Left for Dead, He Crawled 200 Miles
topics.hook: Half-dead, he dragged himself across the wilderness for revenge.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Glass's 1823 ordeal real; distance/details embellished over time.

topics.title: A Spy Who Tricked the Nazis With a Fake Army
topics.hook: He invented agents who never existed and won medals from both sides.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Juan Pujol Garcia ('Garbo'), double agent, D-Day deception.

topics.title: The Deadliest Fire in US History Was Forgotten
topics.hook: It killed more than the Great Chicago Fire — the same night.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1871 Peshtigo Fire; overshadowed by Chicago.

topics.title: America's Worst Maritime Disaster Was Buried in the News
topics.hook: 1,200 died — but it happened the week Lincoln was killed.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1865 Sultana explosion; overshadowed by Lincoln assassination.

topics.title: A Doctor Performed Brain Surgery With an Ice Pick
topics.hook: He did thousands, sometimes in minutes, through the eye.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Walter Freeman's transorbital lobotomy.

topics.title: A Roman Legion of 5,000 Men Disappeared From History
topics.hook: Rome's 9th Legion marched north and stopped existing.
topics.fact_theory_note: THEORY: Legio IX Hispana's fate debated; 'lost in Britain' is popular but contested by historians.

topics.title: The Oldest Map of the Sky Ever Found
topics.hook: A Bronze Age disc showing real stars, 3,600 years old.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Nebra sky disc, ~Bronze Age; dating debated by some but broadly accepted.

topics.title: Killer Whales That Teamed Up With Human Whalers
topics.hook: Orcas herded whales to hunters — for a share of the kill.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 'Law of the Tongue' at Eden, Australia; Old Tom's skeleton displayed.

topics.title: People Were So Afraid of Being Buried Alive They Built Escape Coffins
topics.hook: Bells, air tubes, and 'I'm alive' flags for the dead.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: safety coffins patented in the 18th-19th c. amid premature-burial fears.

topics.title: Five Kids Vanished in a Fire That Left No Bones
topics.hook: The house burned — but the children were never found.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1945 Sodder case; no remains recovered; fate unsolved/contested.

topics.title: Hundreds of Perfect Stone Balls in the Jungle
topics.hook: Who carved them, and why nearly perfect spheres?
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Diquis spheres real; makers known (Diquis), exact purpose THEORY.

topics.title: Ancient Greeks Built a Steam Engine — for Fun
topics.hook: A working steam device, 1,700 years before the Industrial Revolution.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Hero of Alexandria's aeolipile and devices described in his works.

topics.title: Stones So Tight You Can't Fit a Blade Between Them
topics.hook: Giant boulders fitted without mortar.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Inca ashlar masonry at Saksaywaman; techniques partly understood.

topics.title: Upside-Down Temples Dug Hundreds of Feet Down
topics.hook: Stairways descending into the earth for water.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Indian stepwells (e.g., Chand Baori) real and ancient.

topics.title: A Dam Failure That May Have Killed 170,000
topics.hook: Cascading dam collapses erased entire towns.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: 1975 Banqiao failure; death toll estimates vary widely.

topics.title: A Man Whose Memory Lasts Only Seconds
topics.hook: Every moment, he believes he just woke up.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Clive Wearing, amnesia after encephalitis.

topics.title: Europeans Once Ate Mummies as Medicine
topics.hook: Ground-up human remains were sold as a cure.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 'mummia'/corpse medicine well documented in early modern Europe.

topics.title: A Pilot Reported a Strange Object, Then Went Silent
topics.hook: 'It's not an aircraft' — then he and his plane vanished.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Valentich vanished 1978; UFO framing is THEORY, likely spatial disorientation.

topics.title: A Clay Disc Stamped With Symbols No One Can Decode
topics.hook: The world's first 'typed' document is still unreadable.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Phaistos Disc undeciphered; authenticity occasionally questioned (note).

topics.title: A Giant Claw That Lifted Roman Ships Out of the Water
topics.hook: Archimedes built a war machine that flipped warships.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Claw of Archimedes described by ancient sources; tested as plausible.

topics.title: They Crossed a Continent, Then Died Waiting at the Finish Line
topics.hook: Rescue missed them by hours.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Burke and Wills, 1860-61 Australia.

topics.title: An Explorer Was Set Adrift by His Own Crew and Vanished
topics.hook: His men put him in a small boat and sailed home.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Hudson cast adrift 1611; fate unknown.

topics.title: Enemies Played Soccer Together in No Man's Land
topics.hook: On Christmas 1914, the shooting just stopped.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1914 Christmas Truce; football matches attested in some sectors.

topics.title: A 1,600-Year-Old Iron Pillar That Won't Rust
topics.hook: Open-air iron that refuses to corrode.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Iron Pillar of Delhi; corrosion resistance explained by passive film.

topics.title: A Roman Resort Town Sank Beneath the Sea
topics.hook: Marble villas now sit on the seafloor.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Baiae partly submerged by volcanic bradyseism; underwater park exists.

topics.title: Stone Blocks Cut Like They Were Machined
topics.hook: Precise H-blocks that puzzle engineers.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Puma Punku real Tiwanaku site; 'machined' framing exaggerated — note carefully.

topics.title: A Fog So Toxic It Killed Thousands in Days
topics.hook: London's air turned deadly and nobody could see.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1952 Great Smog of London; thousands of excess deaths.

topics.title: Doctors Once Blew Smoke Up People's Backsides to Save Them
topics.hook: It's where the phrase comes from — literally.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: tobacco smoke enemas used for resuscitation, 18th c.

topics.title: Doctors May Have Bled a President to Death
topics.hook: They drained ~40% of his blood trying to cure a sore throat.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Washington's 1799 treatment; bloodletting's role in death is debated.

topics.title: A Ship Crushed by Ice Left Its Crew on the Frozen Sea
topics.hook: They walked across the ice as their ship sank.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Karluk disaster, 1913-16; survival story documented.

topics.title: The Man Who Conquered the Poles Vanished on a Rescue
topics.hook: He flew off to save a rival and was never found.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Amundsen disappeared 1928 during an Arctic rescue flight.

topics.title: A Dead Pig Almost Started a US-UK War
topics.hook: One shot pig nearly triggered an international war in 1859.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1859 San Juan Islands standoff; only casualty was a pig.

topics.title: A War Ended After One Cannonball Hit a Soup Pot
topics.hook: The only shot of the war struck a kettle.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1784 Kettle War, Dutch vs Austrian Netherlands; near-bloodless.

topics.title: The Cloth That Science Still Argues About
topics.hook: A faint image no one can fully explain.
topics.fact_theory_note: CONTESTED: 1988 C-14 dated it medieval; debates continue. Frame as disputed, not proof.

topics.title: A Roman Emperor Tried to Make His Horse a Senator
topics.hook: Caligula gave his horse a house and servants.
topics.fact_theory_note: THEORY: Incitatus 'consul' story from hostile sources; likely satire/exaggeration — frame as claim.

topics.title: A Dog Was Worshipped as a Saint
topics.hook: Medieval villagers prayed to a greyhound.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: cult of Saint Guinefort recorded by Inquisitor Stephen of Bourbon.

topics.title: One Doctor Stopped an Epidemic by Removing a Pump Handle
topics.hook: He mapped the deaths and traced them to a single well.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: John Snow, Broad Street pump, 1854; birth of epidemiology.

topics.title: A War Named After a Severed Ear
topics.hook: A captain's pickled ear helped start a war.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: War of Jenkins' Ear, 1739; the ear-in-Parliament story is partly legend.

topics.title: A 1513 Map That Seems to Show Too Much
topics.hook: A 500-year-old map drawn with strange accuracy.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Piri Reis map real, 1513; 'shows Antarctica' claims are THEORY/overstated.

topics.title: A Volcano in Iceland Helped Starve Europe
topics.hook: An eruption poisoned skies and crops across a continent.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: 1783 Laki eruption; downstream famine/death links partly modeled.

topics.title: A French Expedition Vanished and Took Decades to Find
topics.hook: Two ships sailed the Pacific and simply stopped reporting.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: La Perouse expedition lost ~1788; wrecks later found at Vanikoro.

topics.title: A Disaster That Bankrupted an Entire Nation
topics.hook: Scotland gambled its fortune on a colony and lost everything.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Darien scheme, 1690s; contributed to the 1707 Union.

topics.title: A Famous Writer Rode Into a War and Vanished
topics.hook: He wrote his own ending: 'to be a gringo in Mexico...'
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Bierce disappeared ~1913-14 in Mexico; fate unknown.

topics.title: A 'Found' Missing Boy Wasn't Who Everyone Thought
topics.hook: DNA a century later revealed the wrong child grew up in his place.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1912 case; 2004 DNA showed misidentification.

topics.title: A Man Who Couldn't Make New Memories for 50 Years
topics.hook: Surgery erased his ability to remember anything new.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Henry Molaison (H.M.); foundational memory neuroscience.

topics.title: The Largest Concrete Dome Stood for 1,900 Years
topics.hook: Unreinforced concrete that still holds the record.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Pantheon's dome is the largest unreinforced concrete dome.

topics.title: A Scientist Who Tried to Eat Every Animal on Earth
topics.hook: He claimed to have eaten a king's preserved heart.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Buckland's 'zoophagy' attested; the royal-heart anecdote is traditional.

topics.title: An Arctic Mission Ended in Starvation and Whispered Horrors
topics.hook: Of 25 men, 6 came home — and questions followed.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Greely expedition 1881-84; cannibalism allegations contested.

topics.title: A 600-Man Expedition Ended With 4 Survivors Walking Across a Continent
topics.hook: Eight years lost in the Americas.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Narvaez expedition 1527; Cabeza de Vaca's account survives.

topics.title: A Soccer Match Started a Real War
topics.hook: Two nations went to war after a World Cup qualifier.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1969 El Salvador-Honduras; football was a flashpoint, not sole cause (note tensions).

topics.title: An Army Attacked Itself and Lost Thousands
topics.hook: The Austrian army panicked and fought... itself.
topics.fact_theory_note: THEORY: 1788 Battle of Karansebes; casualty figures and details are disputed/possibly exaggerated.

topics.title: Two Nations Fought 50 Years by Swapping Bottles
topics.hook: Canada and Denmark 'attacked' an island with liquor.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Whisky War over Hans Island; resolved 2022.

topics.title: A Runaway Dog Started a War Between Nations
topics.hook: A soldier chasing his dog sparked an invasion.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: 1925 War of the Stray Dog, Greece-Bulgaria; the dog anecdote is traditional.

topics.title: People Kept Vanishing on the Same Mountain
topics.hook: Several disappeared on Glastenbury — some never found.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: real 1940s Vermont disappearances; 'triangle' link is THEORY/folklore.

topics.title: A Diplomat Walked Around His Horses and Vanished
topics.hook: He stepped behind a coach in 1809 and was gone.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Bathurst disappeared 1809; details embellished over time.

topics.title: A Viking Coin Found in Native American Ruins
topics.hook: An 11th-century Norse coin, thousands of miles from home.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Maine penny is a genuine Norse coin; how it arrived is THEORY (trade vs. hoax).

topics.title: Two Brothers Buried Alive in Their Own Hoard
topics.hook: Booby-trapped tunnels of junk filled their mansion.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Collyer brothers, 1947; ~140 tons removed.

topics.title: A 100m Lighthouse Stood for 1,600 Years
topics.hook: One of the Seven Wonders guided ships with fire and mirrors.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Lighthouse of Alexandria real; exact height/mirror details THEORY.

topics.title: A Nightclub Fire That Changed Safety Laws Forever
topics.hook: 492 died in minutes behind locked exits.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1942 Cocoanut Grove fire; led to safety reforms.

topics.title: A Mountain of Coal Waste Buried a School
topics.hook: 144 died, most of them children, in seconds.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: 1966 Aberfan disaster; colliery spoil tip collapse.

topics.title: An Ancient Egyptian Object Shaped Like a Plane
topics.hook: Did Egyptians understand flight?
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: artifact exists; 'glider' interpretation is THEORY, rejected by most experts.

topics.title: Ancient Tunnels That Carried Water Across Deserts
topics.hook: Persians moved water 50 miles underground by hand.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: qanat/karez systems, millennia old, still used.

topics.title: A Feral Boy Was Raised in a Royal Court
topics.hook: A child found in the woods became a king's pet curiosity.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: Peter the Wild Boy real; modern view: likely Pitt-Hopkins syndrome.

topics.title: France Invaded Mexico Over a Pastry Bill
topics.hook: A baker's unpaid debt led to a French invasion.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: 'Pastry War' 1838; the pastry claim was one pretext among debts.

topics.title: A War Fought Over a Sacred Golden Throne
topics.hook: A British demand to sit on a stool sparked rebellion.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: War of the Golden Stool, 1900, Ashanti vs Britain.

topics.title: Britain and Iceland Fought Wars Over Fish
topics.hook: NATO allies rammed each other's ships over cod.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Cod Wars, mid-20th c.; ship ramming and net-cutting documented.

topics.title: A War That Killed Most of a Country's Men
topics.hook: Paraguay lost up to 70% of its adult men in one war.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT/THEORY: War of the Triple Alliance 1864-70; mortality estimates debated.

topics.title: A Spanish Town Was 'At War' With France for 100 Years
topics.hook: Lijar declared war on France and forgot to stop.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Lijar 'declared war' 1883, symbolically ended 1981.

topics.title: A Vice President's Daughter Vanished at Sea
topics.hook: She boarded a ship in 1813 and was never seen again.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Theodosia Burr Alston lost at sea 1813; fate THEORY.

topics.title: Ohio and Michigan Went to War Over Toledo
topics.hook: Two US states nearly fought over a strip of mud.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Toledo War 1835-36, essentially bloodless.

topics.title: The War Almost Nobody Died In
topics.hook: An entire US army marched on Utah — and barely fought.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Utah War 1857-58, largely bloodless (but note Mountain Meadows separately).

topics.title: A Man So Beloved People Paid Just to Meet Him
topics.hook: Britain's gentle giant became a folk hero.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Daniel Lambert, early 1800s; treat with dignity.

topics.title: The Bloodless War Over a Forest
topics.hook: Two armies massed over trees — nobody died in battle.
topics.fact_theory_note: FACT: Aroostook War 1838-39, no combat deaths.
